metadata.purpose: Flutter e-commerce application composed of FeaturePlugins powered by moose_core.

layers.name: Plugins
layers.description: FeaturePlugin implementations that register widgets, hooks, routes.

layers.name: Sections
layers.description: UI fragments extending FeatureSection; configurable via JSON.

layers.name: BLoC
layers.description: Event-driven logic (Event → Bloc → State). Required for every interactive section.

layers.name: Repositories
layers.description: Abstract data sources defined in moose_core, implemented by adapters.

layers.name: Adapters
layers.description: Backend-specific implementations (Shopify, WooCommerce, custom) that register repository factories via AdapterRegistry.
notes: Adapters must extend BackendAdapter and register repository factories inside initialize().
configSchema is abstract on BackendAdapter — compile error if omitted. Validated before initialize() is called.
MooseBootstrapper handles adapter registration order; adapters are registered before plugins.

steps: Create folder lib/plugins/{plugin_name}/ with subfolders logic/, presentation/, data/ if needed.
Add {plugin_name}_plugin.dart extending FeaturePlugin; implement name, version, onRegister, onInit, getRoutes.
Register FeatureSections and addon/action handlers inside onRegister() (sync). Reserve onInit() for async I/O only.
Each section extends FeatureSection, overrides getDefaultSettings(), uses getSetting<T>().
To access AdapterRegistry inside a FeatureSection's build(), call adaptersOf(context).getRepository<T>() — never use AdapterRegistry() singleton.
All business logic lives in Bloc classes under logic/bloc/** (events, states, blocs).
Expose configuration knobs through assets/config/environment.json and document schema.

Optionally register addon builders inside onRegister() via addonRegistry.register('slot.name:zone', builder, priority: N) to inject supplementary widgets (badges, overlays) into slots exposed by other sections.
Bootstrap the app with MooseBootstrapper(appContext: ctx).run(config:, adapters:, plugins:) and wrap the widget tree with MooseScope(appContext: ctx, child: ...).

must: FeaturePlugin names are unique snake_case strings.
No business logic inside Widgets; always delegate to Bloc.
Use repository interfaces from moose_core; never call http directly.
All numeric UI settings stored as double.
Add docs to doc/ai-ready/PLUGIN_SYSTEM.md when introducing new hooks.
Wrap the widget tree with MooseScope(appContext: ctx) so that context.moose and MooseScope.of() work in all descendant widgets.
Cancel all EventBus subscriptions in onStop() to prevent memory leaks.
mustNot: Do not mutate configuration maps in place; copy before editing.
Do not access adapters directly from widgets; go through repositories.
Do not use singleton constructors for any registry (WidgetRegistry(), HookRegistry(), AddonRegistry(), ActionRegistry(), EventBus(), etc.); all registries are owned by MooseAppContext.
Do not call AdapterRegistry() — use context.moose.adapterRegistry or adaptersOf(context) in FeatureSection.build().
Do not throw exceptions from AddonRegistry builders; return null to skip rendering gracefully.
Do not use AddonRegistry to own a full screen area; use WidgetRegistry for that.
Do not call ConfigManager() directly — use appContext.configManager or context.moose.configManager.
Do not register sections inside onInit() — use onRegister() for all synchronous registration.
Do not call CacheManager.initPersistentCache() manually — MooseBootstrapper.run() handles this.
Do not add hookRegistry or eventBus constructor parameters to CoreRepository subclasses — CoreRepository has no fields; only pass what your concrete class actually needs.

codeStyle: Keep files ASCII unless localization requires otherwise.
aiSpecific: Always cite doc/ai-ready/* reference when deviating from defaults.
When unsure, fall back to doc/ai-ready/ANTI_PATTERNS.md and doc/ai-ready/README.md.

templates.featurePlugin: class {ClassName} extends FeaturePlugin { @override String get name => '{plugin}'; @override String get version => '1.0.0'; @override Map<String, dynamic> getDefaultSettings() => {}; @override void onRegister() { widgetRegistry.register('{plugin}.section', (context, {data, onEvent}) => {SectionWidget}(settings: data?['settings'] as Map<String, dynamic>? ?? {})); } @override Future<void> onInit() async { /* async I/O only */ } @override Map<String, WidgetBuilder>? getRoutes() => null; }
templates.featureSection: class {SectionWidget} extends FeatureSection { const {SectionWidget}({super.key, super.settings}); @override Map<String, dynamic> getDefaultSettings() => {'title': 'Section Title'}; @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { final title = getSetting<String>('title'); final repo = adaptersOf(context).getRepository<{RepositoryType}>(); return Text(title); } }
templates.addonRegistration: @override void onRegister() { addonRegistry.register('{component}.{widget}:{slot}', (context, {data, onEvent}) { final item = data?['{dataKey}'] as {Type}?; if (item == null || !{condition}) return null; return {AddonWidget}(); }, priority: 10); }
templates.addonSlotRender: final addons = context.moose.addonRegistry.build('{component}.{widget}:{slot}', context, data: {'{dataKey}': item}); if (addons.isNotEmpty) Column(children: addons)
templates.appBootstrap: void main() async { WidgetsFlutterBinding.ensureInitialized(); final ctx = MooseAppContext(); runApp(MooseScope(appContext: ctx, child: MaterialApp(home: _BootstrapScreen(appContext: ctx)))); } // In _BootstrapScreen.initState: await MooseBootstrapper(appContext: ctx).run(config: config, adapters: [...], plugins: [() => MyPlugin()]);
templates.bloc: class {Feature}Event extends Equatable { const {Feature}Event(); @override List<Object?> get props => []; } class {Feature}State extends Equatable { const {Feature}State(); @override List<Object?> get props => []; } class {Feature}Bloc extends Bloc<{Feature}Event, {Feature}State> { {Feature}Bloc(this._repository) : super(const {Feature}State()) { on<{Feature}Event>(_onEvent); } final {Repository}Type _repository; Future<void> _onEvent({Feature}Event event, Emitter<{Feature}State> emit) async {} }

coreClasses: MooseAppContext (moose_core/lib/src/app/moose_app_context.dart) — owns all registries; create one per app; all fields injectable for testing
MooseScope (moose_core/lib/src/app/moose_scope.dart) — InheritedWidget providing MooseAppContext to widget tree; access via context.moose or MooseScope.of(context)
MooseBootstrapper (moose_core/lib/src/app/moose_bootstrapper.dart) — orchestrates config, cache init, adapter, and plugin initialization; returns BootstrapReport with timings and failures map

FeaturePlugin (moose_core/lib/src/plugin/feature_plugin.dart) — receives injected appContext; convenience getters delegate to appContext; lifecycle: onRegister()[sync] → onInit()[async] → onStart()[async] → onAppLifecycle() → onStop()
FeatureSection (moose_core/lib/src/widgets/feature_section.dart) — use adaptersOf(context) to access AdapterRegistry inside build(); NOT a getter on the class
WidgetRegistry (moose_core/lib/src/widgets/widget_registry.dart) — registers full FeatureSections by name key; register in onRegister()

AddonRegistry (moose_core/lib/src/widgets/addon_registry.dart) — slot-based supplementary widgets; register in onRegister(); builders may return null; multiple builders per slot in descending priority order
HookRegistry (moose_core/lib/src/events/hook_registry.dart) — synchronous data-transformation hooks; built-in hook: bottom_tabs:filter_tabs

ActionRegistry (moose_core/lib/src/actions/action_registry.dart) — handles UserInteraction dispatched by type (UserInteractionType: internal, external, custom, none); factory methods: UserInteraction.internal(), .external(), .custom(), .none()
CoreRepository (moose_core/lib/src/repositories/repository.dart) — base class with void initialize() {}; no constructor params; concrete subclasses declare only what they need

Repository interfaces (moose_core/lib/repositories.dart) — ProductsRepository, CartRepository, AuthRepository, SearchRepository, ReviewRepository, PostRepository, BannerRepository, PushNotificationRepository, ShortsRepository, StoreRepository, LocationRepository
configPathRules: Plugin settings path includes 'settings' segment: plugins:{name}:settings:{key}
Adapter settings path has NO 'settings' segment: adapters:{name}:{key}
Both '.' and ':' work as path separators — they are interchangeable
Use getSetting<T>(key) inside FeaturePlugin — resolves plugins:{name}:settings:{key} automatically

cliCommands.description: Scaffold an empty Flutter project wired to moose_core.

flags.--template <name>: Scaffold from a built-in template (e.g. 'shopify'). Mutually exclusive with --manifest.
flags.--manifest <path|url>: Scaffold from a custom manifest file or HTTPS URL.
flags.--configurations <path=value>: Pre-fill environment.json values at scaffold time. Repeatable.

flags.--git <repo>: Install from a remote git repository.
Install from a remote git repository.

cliCommands.description: Create a new ARB localization file for the given locale code.
Show detailed usage for any moose_cli command.

pageSystem.description: Decision guide for adding pages and routes. Three forms exist — choose based on criteria below. Full guide: doc/ai-ready/PAGE_SYSTEM.md

decisionTree.description: Answer each question in order. Stop at the first 'yes' and use the recommended form.

questions.question: Does the screen need a BLoC or repository instance set up at route creation time?
Does the screen need navigation arguments (e.g. productId, postId) at build time?
Do you need multiple routes backed by the same plugin logic (different filters, different section layouts, different settings per route)?

PLAIN_AUTO_ROUTE.summary: No Dart code needed. Bootstrapper registers route and renders sections via PageScreen.
typicalUse: Promotional pages with only section widgets

PLUGIN_OWNED_ROUTE.summary: Plugin calls getRoutes() and returns WidgetBuilder. Set 'plugin':'<name>' in env.json entry — bootstrapper stores it under 'plugin:<name>:<route>' and skips route registration. Plugin registers route itself.
PLUGIN_OWNED_ROUTE.dartChange: Add route to getRoutes() in plugin class

PAGE_SLOT.summary: Plugin declares pageSlots getter with PageSlotBuilder. Bootstrapper registers each env.json route that references the pageSlotIdentifier. One slot can back unlimited routes.
PAGE_SLOT.pageSlotBuilderSignature: Widget Function(BuildContext context, Map<String, dynamic> pageConfig, Map<String, dynamic> settings, Object? routeArgs)
PAGE_SLOT.dartChange: Override pageSlots getter; clean up BLoCs in onStop()
typicalUse: Product list pages with different per-route filters
Product detail page (needs productId from routeArgs)

appBarButtonWidgets.description: Cart icon with item count badge

availableSlots.description: Filterable product list screen. settings.filters = ProductFilters fields (categoryId, onSale, orderBy, order, tag, search, perPage). routeArgs not used.
Product detail screen. Requires productId in routeArgs (String or Map{productId:String}). Each productId gets its own BLoC.

products.description: Home-page product list/carousel. Settings: filters(object), title(string), layout(string).
Product grid. Requires ProductsBloc context. Settings: columns(int).
Add-to-cart / buy-now bar. Use as bottomBar.

banner.description: Auto-play banner carousel. Settings: key(string, required), height(double), autoPlay(bool), showIndicators(bool).

blog.description: Latest posts list or carousel. Settings: title(string), limit(int), filter(string), layout(list|carousel).

pageExamples.name: Brand Story / About Page
pageExamples.scenario: Static marketing page with hero banner and blog posts. No BLoC, no navigation arguments.
pageExamples.reasoning: No state setup or runtime args needed. Zero Dart code required.

pageExamples.name: User Profile Page
pageExamples.scenario: Loads authenticated user orders and settings. Needs AuthRepository and a ProfileBloc wired at route creation. Single route.
pageExamples.reasoning: Needs BLoC wiring at screen creation. Single route — no multi-instance requirement.

pageExamples.name: Multiple Category Product Lists
pageExamples.scenario: /products, /products/new, /products/sale — same list screen and BLoC, different filters and appBar titles per route.
pageExamples.reasoning: Same plugin logic, multiple config-driven instances. Adding a new list route requires only environment.json.
pageExamples.dartSketch: // No new Dart needed if products plugin already declares 'plugins/products/slots/product_list'.

pageExamples.name: Product Detail Page (needs productId)
pageExamples.scenario: /products/item and /products/electronics show the same product with different section layouts. productId only known at navigation time.
pageExamples.reasoning: routeArgs needed for productId at route-build time. Multiple routes with different section arrays.

pageExamples.name: Seasonal Campaign Landing Pages
pageExamples.scenario: /summer-sale, /black-friday — each with a different hero banner, different product sections, custom appBar title. Layouts changeable via environment.json only.
pageExamples.reasoning: Multiple routes with completely different sections, all backed by the same ProductsBloc. Config drives everything. No routeArgs needed.
pageExamples.dartSketch: // No new Dart code. Both routes reuse the existing 'plugins/products/slots/product_list' handler.

pitfalls: Adding 'plugin':'<name>' to a slot entry causes the bootstrapper to store it under 'plugin:<name>:<route>' and skip route registration — the route will 404.
Calling ModalRoute.of(context) in the outer WidgetBuilder returns null. routeArgs is already extracted and forwarded to PageSlotBuilder by the bootstrapper.
Not closing BLoCs created inside slot handlers in onStop() causes memory leaks.
Not guarding BLoC creation with containsKey causes duplicate network requests on every rebuild.

addAddon: 1. Decide the slot name using the convention '{component}.{widget}:{slot}' (e.g., 'product.card:badge').
2. In the plugin that owns the UI widget, call context.moose.addonRegistry.build('product.card:badge', context, data: {'product': product}) and render the returned list.
3. In any plugin that wants to inject content, register inside onRegister(): addonRegistry.register('product.card:badge', (context, {data, onEvent}) { ... return null or Widget; }, priority: 10).
4. Return null from the builder when the addon should not appear (e.g., product is not on sale).
5. Higher priority value = rendered first. Default priority is 1.

l10n.description: Runtime two-layer localization system. Plugins register English defaults; LocalizationPlugin overlays per-locale JSON files. Full guide: doc/ai-ready/L10N.md
lookupOrder: 2. English default registered by the plugin via registerDefaults()
4. The key string itself (prevents blank UI)
l10n.rebuildMechanism: MooseL10n extends ChangeNotifier. MooseScope listens to l10n and passes l10nGeneration to _MooseScopeInherited. Every context.moose dependant rebuilds automatically on setLocale() or applyOverrides().

pluginRegistration.where: onRegister() — always before any other registration; never in onInit()

widgetUsage.configStringResolve: MooseL10n.resolve(value, context.moose.l10n)  // transparent: returns translation if key registered, else value as-is

hooks.l10n:translate: Registered by LocalizationPlugin. data: String key OR Map{key, fallback?, params?}. Returns translated string.
hooks.l10n:get_locale: Registered by LocalizationPlugin. Returns active locale code as String (e.g. 'ja').

overrideFiles.format: Flat map of fully-qualified key → translated string. No nesting.
rules: Only include keys that differ from English — missing keys fall back silently.
Preserve {placeholder} markers exactly as they appear in the English default.
English apps need no JSON file — plugin defaults cover everything.

localizationPlugin.registrationOrder: Must come after LocaleManagerPlugin in main.dart plugins list.
localizationPlugin.startup: Reads initial locale from moose.locale.hook.get_locale, calls setLocale() + loads JSON file.
localizationPlugin.onLocaleChange: setLocale() → instant rebuild with English defaults (first rebuild); _loadOverrides() async → applyOverrides() → second rebuild with translated strings.

configStringResolution.description: Any string setting from environment.json can transparently be either a literal or an l10n key. Use MooseL10n.resolve() at render time.
configStringResolution.howItWorks: MooseL10n.resolve(value, l10n) calls l10n(value). If result != value, a translation was found. Otherwise returns value unchanged.
examples: MooseL10n.resolve('tabs.home', l10n)  // → 'Home' or 'ホーム' depending on locale
MooseL10n.resolve('MY STORE', l10n)   // → 'MY STORE' (no key match, pass-through)

addingANewLanguage: 1. Create assets/l10n/<localeCode>.json with flat key→value map (only keys that differ from English).
2. Ensure assets/l10n/ is listed under flutter.assets in pubspec.yaml.
3. Add the locale code to LocaleManagerPlugin settings.supportedLocales in environment.json.
4. No Dart code changes needed — LocalizationPlugin loads the file automatically.
commonMistakes: Calling registerDefaults() in onInit() instead of onRegister() — other plugins may query strings before onInit() runs.
Using plugin-prefixed keys in registerDefaults() — prefix is added automatically; 'myPlugin.title' becomes 'myPlugin.myPlugin.title'.
Nesting keys in JSON override file — MooseL10n expects a flat map.
Omitting {placeholder} in translated string — interpolation silently produces empty substitutions.
Not adding LocalizationPlugin after LocaleManagerPlugin in main.dart.
Calling context.moose.l10n() during plugin onRegister() and expecting translated strings — overrides load during onInit(), so only English defaults are available at registration time.
